skill.name: sleek-design-mobile-apps
skill.description: Design mobile app screens through the Sleek API, including project creation, chat-based UI generation, component listing, and screenshot delivery.
skill.summary: Create and iterate on mobile app UI screens in Sleek from natural language prompts.
skill.author: sleekdotdesign

security_audit.summary: Static external command and weak cryptography findings are false positives caused by Markdown backticks, endpoint examples, and inline API terms in SKILL.md. The confirmed risks are intentional network access to https://sleek.design, use of SLEEK_API_KEY, and optional image URL fetching by Sleek servers, so the skill is publishable with a network and credential warning.

medium_findings.title: Authorized External API Access
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to send project names, prompts, component IDs, and screenshots requests to https://sleek.design. This is core functionality, but users should know their design content leaves the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly documents REST API calls to a single external host with user-provided design prompts and optional images. The behavior is intentional and scoped, but it is still real network disclosure.

medium_findings.title: API Key Required For Bearer Authentication
medium_findings.description: The skill requires SLEEK_API_KEY and uses it in Authorization headers for Sleek API calls. This is legitimate credential use, but key scope and storage should be handled carefully.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The environment variable and Authorization header are directly documented. The key is sent only to Sleek endpoints in the instructions, so this is a controlled but sensitive capability.

low_findings.title: External Command Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many inline backticks and fenced examples as Ruby or shell backtick execution. SKILL.md is documentation and contains HTTP examples, endpoint paths, field names, and Markdown tables, not executable Ruby code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line review shows Markdown code formatting and HTTP request examples, not a script interpreter or command execution API. No executable files or shell invocations were present in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code. The referenced lines contain YAML metadata, API documentation, text examples, or screenshot option names.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed lines do not define hashing, encryption, random number generation, or cryptographic verification. The alerts appear to be token-level matches inside prose and API examples.

low_findings.title: Image URL Fetching Has Privacy Implications
low_findings.description: The skill allows imageUrls in chat messages and states those URLs are fetched by Sleek servers. This is documented, but users should avoid private or sensitive URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The behavior is clearly documented and may be legitimate visual context handling. The risk depends on what URLs users provide, so this is a lower-severity privacy concern.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance alerts map to documentation about image URLs, screenshot dot-grid options, and HTTP error codes. No host probing, environment enumeration, or local system discovery instructions were found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced text is API documentation and rendering configuration. It does not instruct the agent to inspect local systems or enumerate network targets.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Is Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The static analyzer combined code execution, network, and credential signals into a critical heuristic. Context review found no executable code path; the network and credential use are documented Sleek API operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The heuristic depends on a code execution signal, but reviewed evidence is Markdown and HTTP documentation only. Network and API key use remain real but scoped to the described vendor API.

content.value_statement: Mobile UI work often stalls when teams need quick visual screens from rough ideas. This skill connects Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to Sleek so users can create projects, generate screens, and view screenshots.
actual_capabilities: Creates Sleek projects through the documented projects endpoint.
Sends natural language design requests to Sleek chat runs.
Polls chat run status until a design generation finishes or fails.
Lists project components so users can target existing screens.
Requests PNG or WebP screenshots for one or more generated screens.
Guides safe retry behavior with idempotency keys and conflict handling.
limitations: Requires a Sleek Pro+ account and a SLEEK_API_KEY environment variable.
Sends design prompts, project data, and optional image URLs to Sleek.
Does not generate local source code for native iOS or Android apps.
Only documents the Sleek API workflow and does not include executable helper scripts.

use_cases.title: Prototype a New App Concept
use_cases.description: Create a Sleek project from a short product idea and receive generated mobile screens for review.

use_cases.title: Iterate on Existing Screens
use_cases.description: Target a specific screen, send a focused change request, and review updated screenshots.

use_cases.title: Prepare Visual Design Reviews
use_cases.description: Generate individual and combined screenshots so stakeholders can compare screen states before implementation.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple App Concept
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a new Sleek project for a habit tracking app and generate the first set of mobile screens.

prompt_templates.title: Refine a Generated Screen
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the settings screen in my Sleek project, make it cleaner and easier to scan, then show the updated screenshot.

prompt_templates.title: Use Visual References
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this HTTPS image reference to guide the style of a finance dashboard screen in Sleek.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Full Review Flow
prompt_templates.prompt: List my Sleek projects, choose the travel app project, update all onboarding screens, poll until complete, and deliver individual plus combined screenshots.

output_examples.input: Design a meal planning mobile app for busy parents.
output_examples.output: Creates a Sleek project, sends the design request, waits for completion, then returns screenshots for the generated screens.

output_examples.input: Update the profile screen to feel more premium.
output_examples.output: Finds the target component, sends a focused edit request, and provides a fresh screenshot of the updated screen.

output_examples.input: Show every new screen after generation.
output_examples.output: Returns individual screenshots for each new screen and one combined screenshot containing all project screens.

best_practices: Use the narrowest Sleek API key scopes needed for the requested task.
Provide clear product goals, target users, and visual constraints in each design prompt.
Review screenshots after each completed chat run before requesting more changes.
anti_patterns: Do not pass private or sensitive image URLs as visual references.
Do not send another chat message before the active project run finishes.
Do not use broad API key scopes when only screenshots or read access are needed.

faq.question: Does this skill require an API key?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires SLEEK_API_KEY and a Sleek plan with API access.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does the skill send data outside my environment?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends requests to https://sleek.design to create projects, run design chats, and render screenshots.

faq.question: Can it edit a specific screen?
faq.answer: Yes. It can list components, select a screen ID, and send a targeted edit request.

faq.question: Does it create production app code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on mobile UI design screens and screenshots inside Sleek.

faq.question: How should image references be handled?
faq.answer: Use only HTTPS image URLs and avoid URLs that contain private or sensitive content.
